events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: replace `any` with a narrow, explicit input type. For example, if this helper expects a JSON object, use `data: Record<string, unknown>` (or a dedicated interface if the shape is known) and update call sites accordingly. This keeps boundary types safe while still allowing runtime validation.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: wrap `JSON.parse` in a safe parser and return a typed result. Example approach: `function tryParseJson<T>(raw: string): { ok: true; data: T } | { ok: false; error: Error } { try { return { ok: true, data: JSON.parse(raw) as T } } catch (error) { return { ok: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error : new Error("Invalid JSON") } } }`. Then handle the `ok: false` branch instead of letting parse throw.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Concrete fix: implement an actual predicate based on required fields/types (or remove the function if unused). Example: `function isValidUser(input: unknown): input is { id: string; email: string } { if (!input || typeof input !== "object") return false; const obj = input as Record<string, unknown>; return typeof obj.id === "string" && typeof obj.email === "string" && obj.email.includes("@"); }`. This turns validation into a meaningful runtime guard.

events.content:
Done — I reviewed PR `#433` and replied to each unresolved review thread only (3 total), each with a concrete fix suggestion:

- `src/utils/mixed-helpers.ts:14` — suggested replacing `any` with a narrowed explicit type/interface.
- `src/utils/mixed-helpers.ts:18` — suggested a safe `JSON.parse` wrapper with typed result/error branch.
- `src/utils/mixed-helpers.ts:22` — suggested implementing a real type-guard predicate (or removing unused validator).

I did not push or modify any code.
